artifacts.claimText: The University of Florence has issued AI guidance for teaching and study activities, with the rectoral decree stating that the guidance is emanated and the governance extract recording favorable action on adoption.

citation.citationTitle: Linee di indirizzo sull'uso dell'Intelligenza Artificiale nella didattica e per attivita di studio
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Under the University of Florence AI teaching/study guidance, instructors may set course-level policies authorizing or prohibiting AI tool use, while remaining within the general principles in the guidance.

citation.citationTitle: Linee di indirizzo sull'uso dell'Intelligenza Artificiale nella didattica e per attivita di studio
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Florence guidance says students may use AI only for purposes and modes explicitly indicated by the instructor for the course activity; if the instructor gives no indication, AI use for the required course activity is not permitted.

citation.citationTitle: Linee di indirizzo sull'uso dell'Intelligenza Artificiale nella didattica e per attivita di studio
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Florence guidance treats improper reliance on AI to avoid student responsibility as an academic integrity issue and says presenting work made entirely or largely by generative AI as one's own is plagiarism.

citation.citationTitle: Linee di indirizzo sull'uso dell'Intelligenza Artificiale nella didattica e per attivita di studio
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For teaching purposes, the University of Florence guidance says personal or confidential data must never be supplied or disseminated through prompting, file uploads, or credential sharing.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Linee di indirizzo sull'uso dell'Intelligenza Artificiale nella didattica e per attivita di studio
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Gli studenti possono utilizzare l'IA solo per gli scopi e secondo le modalita esplicitamente indicate dal docente relativamente alle attivita didattiche previste dal corso. Se il docente non da nessuna indicazione sull'utilizzo delle tecnologie di IA per le attivita didattiche del corso, cio significa che il loro uso per lo svolgimento delle attivita didattiche previste non e consentito.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students may use AI only as explicitly indicated by the instructor; absent instructions means AI use for the course activity is not permitted.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Linee di indirizzo sull'uso dell'Intelligenza Artificiale nella didattica e per attivita di studio
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Qualsiasi utilizzo dell'IA generativa che comporti un aggiramento delle proprie responsabilita nello studio e considerato una violazione dell'integrita accademica. Gli studenti devono essere consapevoli che presentare come proprio un lavoro realizzato interamente o in gran parte dall'IA generativa rappresenta una forma di plagio.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Improper AI use that avoids student responsibility is an academic integrity violation; presenting AI-made work as one's own is plagiarism.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a staged candidate because official UniFi sources document issuance and adoption context; not marked as human reviewed or canonical.
Approved with scope limited to course-level policies set by instructors within the UniFi guidance.
Approved because the source uses explicit permission language for students and states the consequence of no instructor indication.
Approved as a narrow academic-integrity claim tied to the guidance's student responsibility and plagiarism language.
Approved because the source uses mandatory language on personal or confidential data in prompts, file uploads, and credential sharing.

artifacts.title: University of Florence AI Teaching and Study Guidance
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl found official University of Florence guidance on AI use in teaching and study activities. Claims are scoped to the guidance: instructors set course-level AI use rules, students may use AI only as explicitly indicated for course activities, improper reliance can be an academic integrity issue, and personal or confidential data must not be entered into AI prompts, file uploads, or credential sharing.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No broader central binding AI policy beyond the official teaching and study guidance was found in this staged crawl.
Course-level and news sources were not used as standalone policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
